title: Racette Lab: Promoting Cardiometabolic Health & Healthspan
keywords: study #2 acute exercise ± anti-amyloid mechanistic study
study #4 multi-modality healthspan interventions (multi-baric + ai-driven exercise + recovery modalities)

summary.hook: This poster is a lab portfolio overview summarizing four ongoing/related research areas in the Racette Lab focused on cardiometabolic health, Alzheimer’s disease-related interventions, Native American cardiovascular health, and healthspan-extension approaches.
summary.summary_30s: This poster is a lab portfolio overview summarizing four ongoing/related research areas in the Racette Lab focused on cardiometabolic health, Alzheimer’s disease-related interventions, Native American cardiovascular health, and healthspan-extension approaches. It highlights (1) a time-restricted eating feasibility trial in mild cognitive impairment (MCI) with a ClinicalTrials.gov identifier, (2) a study of acute aerobic exercise effects with/without anti-amyloid therapy in MCI due to Alzheimer’s disease, (3) cardiovascular health measurement work in Native American youth/adults using CIMT and DXA, and (4) an “XPRIZE Healthspan” effort including multi-baric therapy and AI-driven exercise.
talktrack_2m: Focus: Portfolio-level: Promote cardiometabolic health and healthspan through multiple studies/interventions and collaborations.

Approach: Population: Not stated on poster (mentions adults with MCI; patients with MCI due to Alzheimer’s disease; Native American youth & adults). Primary outcome: Not stated on poster (aims mention feasibility and preliminary effects; acute physiological pathways; cardiovascular health; biological age/healthspan).
Finding: This poster primarily presents aims, methods, sites, collaborators, and study identifiers rather than numeric outcomes.

Why it matters: Authors’ stated conclusions: Not stated on poster (this is a portfolio overview; panels state aims/methods and point to other posters for results). Implications (poster-grounded): The lab’s program integrates dietary timing (TRE), exercise physiology, cardiovascular imaging/body composition, and multi-modality “healthspan” interventions, with collaborations across ASU and Barrow Neurological Institute. Mechanism/explanation (ONLY if stated): Study #1 and Study #2 include mechanistic models linking interventions (TRE or exercise ± anti-amyloid therapy) to pathways relevant to Alzheimer’s disease (details are diagrammatic; full text not expanded). Connections to prior work (ONLY if referenced): Not stated on poster
key_numbers: Not stated on poster (XPRIZE described as 7-year competition).
Study #1 TREAD Trial is registered as ClinicalTrials.gov NCT06429124.
Study #4 references XPRIZE Healthspan as a 7-year, $101 million global competition and lists intervention modalities used by the ASU team.
It highlights (1) a time-restricted eating feasibility trial in mild cognitive impairment (MCI) with a ClinicalTrials.gov identifier, (2) a study of acute aerobic exercise effec...
takeaways: This poster primarily presents aims, methods, sites, collaborators, and study identifiers rather than numeric outcomes.
Study #1 reports “Study Design & Preliminary Results” but directs viewers to “See poster by Jordan Gunning, PhD” for details.
Study #4 describes XPRIZE Healthspan as a 7-year, $101 million global competition and lists intervention methods used by the ASU team.
limitations: The poster does not list formal study limitations.
The poster leaves open this clarification: For Study #1 (TREAD), what were the preliminary feasibility metrics and cardiometabolic/cognitive outcomes?

what_to_ask.question: The TREAD panel mentions “Study Design & Preliminary Results”—what are the top preliminary signals you can share (feasibility, adherence, safety, early endpoints)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This gets the presenter to interpret the main result instead of repeating the title.

what_to_ask.question: What are the main confounders you account for in Study #3 (e.g., age, sex, socioeconomic factors, baseline activity) when interpreting CIMT and body composition?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This probes confidence, assumptions, or limitations without sounding hostile.

what_to_ask.question: If TRE is feasible in MCI, what would be the path to scaling it clinically (dietitian support, digital coaching)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This opens a useful conversation about why the work matters or where it goes next.
